name: auth
context: Persist and reuse authenticated Playwright browser state safely with setup projects, storageState files, roles, cookies, and secrets hygiene.
info: Authentication in Playwright is usually handled by logging in once, saving cookies and localStorage to a storage state JSON file, and reusing that file in tests or scripts. This context covers setup projects, multi-role state, API-assisted login, per-worker accounts, session expiry, secret handling, and how to keep auth artifacts out of git. Agents use it when testing protected pages, automating authenticated research, or avoiding repeated UI login flows in CI.
triggers: do not repeat login in tests

sections.title: Setup project
sections.body: A setup project runs before dependent browser projects. It performs UI login, waits for a post-login condition, then saves `page.context().storageState({ path })`. Dependent projects set `use.storageState` to that path. This avoids repeating login in every test and makes failures easier to diagnose.

sections.title: Multiple roles
sections.body: Create separate storage state files for roles such as admin, user, and viewer: `playwright/.auth/admin.json`, `user.json`, and `viewer.json`. Define separate setup tests or an API-assisted setup that writes each file. Use projects or fixtures to choose the right state per test. Never share one account across parallel tests if the app mutates shared user state.

sections.title: API-assisted login
sections.body: When the app supports API login, use Playwright's `request` fixture or `request.newContext()` to authenticate faster than the UI, then save cookies or issue tokens into a browser context. Still keep at least one UI login test so the login page itself remains covered.

sections.title: Secret hygiene
sections.body: Storage state may include cookies, localStorage tokens, CSRF values, and user identifiers. Keep `playwright/.auth/` in `.gitignore`, avoid uploading auth state as CI artifacts, rotate credentials if state files are committed, and scope test accounts to the minimum permissions needed.

related.note: Both require careful token and secret handling.
